title: [OpenVINO backend] fix ops.array
body:
Fixes a failure when using the OpenVINO backend where keras.ops.array is called with a tensor already produced by the OpenVINO backend (OpenVINOKerasTensor). The existing path could try to convert an OpenVINOKerasTensor to a NumPy array, which is inappropriate for OpenVINO tensors and breaks downstream usage (e.g., RandomSampler with logits in keras_hub tests).

What changed:
- Adjusted the array function to detect OpenVINOKerasTensor inputs and pass them through unchanged instead of attempting a NumPy conversion.
- This keeps OpenVINO tensors as OpenVINO tensors in symbolic/graph contexts and avoids type/value mismatches in tests that rely on OpenVINO tensors as inputs.

Impact:
- Minimal surface area, targeted to OpenVINO-backed paths for ops.array.
- No change to external API behavior for non-OpenVINO backends.
- Should resolve the failing test path in keras_hub RandomSampler test where logits is an OpenVINOKerasTensor.

step_plan.summary: Fixes an OpenVINO-specific edge case in keras.ops.numpy.array by bypassing NumPy conversion when given an OpenVINOKerasTensor, preserving OpenVINO tensor semantics in graph contexts.

steps.operation: Guard OpenVINOKerasTensor inputs in ops.array and return input unchanged
steps.reason: To avoid converting OpenVINO-produced tensors to NumPy arrays, which breaks OpenVINO tensor semantics and downstream usage in graph contexts and tests.
steps.side_effects: OpenVINO-specific paths will bypass NumPy conversion; non-OpenVINO backends remain unaffected; may require re-running OpenVINO-focused tests (e.g., keras_hub RandomSampler) to verify the fix.
